mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its long-term trend reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during supply-led expansions.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during demand-constrained regimes, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Falling inflation combined with stable or declining policy rates confirms disinflationary easing, triggering rapid positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days as real yields compress.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing, or 5-day returns remain flat when CPIAUCSL declines but FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.0% signals severe demand contraction and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings estimates are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or the threshold fails to separate bull and bear market phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate supply chain bottlenecks and factory shutdowns, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days during inventory correction phases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when supply shocks are offset by fiscal stimulus, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling windows.
